name: ky (Tiny HTTP Client for Fetch API)

auth.notes: HTTP client utility; no auth (pass headers manually)

performance.notes: Thin wrapper over native fetch; minimal overhead over bare fetch

agent_readiness.notes: Tiny HTTP client built on fetch. ky.get(url).json(), .text(), .blob(). Retry with exponential backoff. Hooks (beforeRequest, afterResponse, beforeError). Timeout support. Throws on non-2xx (HTTPError with response). JSON shortcut: ky.post(url, {json: data}). Extend with ky.create({prefixUrl, headers}). 3.4KB minzipped (much smaller than axios). ESM-only (Sindre Sorhus). Browser + Node 18+. Alternative: got is Node-only but more features. axios has broader legacy support. Wretch is similar but different API style.

gotchas: Less feature-rich than got (no streams, less middleware)
No interceptors per se — uses hooks instead
best_when: Modern browser and Node.js 18+ projects needing lightweight HTTP
Projects already using native fetch wanting ergonomics
avoid_when: Node 16 or older (no native fetch support)
Need streaming responses or advanced HTTP features (use got)
summary: ky is a tiny (3.4KB) HTTP client built on native fetch. ky.get(url).json() with retry, timeout, hooks, and automatic error throwing on non-2xx. JSON shortcut for POST/PUT. ESM-only, Node 18+, and browser. Much smaller than axios with a cleaner API. Best for modern TypeScript/ESM projects needing lightweight HTTP with retry logic. Use got for Node-specific advanced features or axios for legacy compatibility.
